description: Canonical conformance corpus. Every file is a valid v0.1 OpenTrajectory document (spec §7). Each demonstrates one shape an adapter must get right. `must` are machine-checked invariants (conformance/check.mjs) so a case can never silently stop demonstrating what it claims.

cases.demonstrates: The floor: only the five required fields, zero steps, status unknown. The smallest conformant document.
Secret redaction by construction: the value is replaced with [REDACTED] and the nearest redacted:true is set (spec §4).
A failed run: a tool_call with success:false + error, a give_up decision, outcome.status failure / resolved false.
A real multi-step run: a plan decision then several tool_calls (Grep -> Edit -> Bash), monotonic 0-based indexes, timing + cost.
An evaluator-filled outcome.verdict (the eval-first field telemetry formats lack): diagnosis, confidence, offending_step_index, evaluator.
Claude Code native shape normalized: tool_use/tool_result, toolu_* ids.
Codex CLI native shape normalized: function_call/function_call_output, call_id, shell_command.
Gemini CLI native shape normalized: typed log items, run_shell_command, token usage.

LangGraph/LangSmith run tree normalized: nested runs by run_type, a tool run carrying an error. Adapter validated on real LangSmith exports (SDK wrap_openai fixtures, single-LLM); a real multi-tool agent trace is still synthetic-only.
